meta.title: Unit 2 — Common & Proper Nouns (Test 5)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Advanced practice: identify errors and classify nouns from a variety of contexts.

questions.question: Which word is a proper noun?
questions.explanation: "Taipei" is a proper noun — the specific name of a city.

questions.question: "Hospital" is a common noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "hospital" refers to any hospital — it's a common noun.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Saturday"? (common or proper)
questions.hint: Days of the week are always capitalized
questions.explanation: Saturday is a proper noun — days of the week must always be capitalized.

questions.question: Which of these is a proper noun?
questions.explanation: "December" is a proper noun — it's a month name and must be capitalized.

questions.question: "sunday" is correctly written.
questions.explanation: Sunday is a proper noun (day of the week) — must be capitalized: Sunday.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Children's Day"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: Children's Day is a proper noun — specific holiday names must be capitalized.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: park/hospital/teacher = general words (common). Taipei/December = specific names (proper).

questions.question: In the sentence "Daan Park is big," the capitalization is correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! "Daan Park" is a proper noun (specific park name) — both words are correctly capitalized.

questions.question: Which sentence has correct capitalization?
options: We wear red for chinese new year.
We wear red for Chinese new year.
We wear red for Chinese New Year.
We wear red for chinese New Year.
questions.explanation: "Chinese New Year" is a proper noun (holiday name) — all words must be capitalized.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Teacher's Day"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: Teacher's Day is a proper noun — a specific holiday name that must be capitalized.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "Daan Park is big." Daan Park is a proper noun.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: school/newspaper = general words (common). Children's Day/Halloween/Saturday = specific names (proper).

questions.question: "August" is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! August is a month name — months are proper nouns and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which word in this sentence is a proper noun? "Ms. Lee is my teacher."
questions.explanation: "Ms. Lee" is a proper noun — it's a specific person's name.

questions.question: Fix this sentence: 'we love going to jiufen village.' What is the correct capitalization of the place?
questions.hint: Both words in a place name need capitals
questions.explanation: "Jiufen Village" is a proper noun — capitalize both words.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "Ms. Lee is my teacher." Ms. Lee is a proper noun.

questions.question: Which word in this list is a common noun that does NOT need a capital letter?
questions.explanation: "school" is a common noun — it does NOT need a capital unless it starts a sentence.

questions.question: The sentence "I have a class every monday" has an error.
questions.explanation: "monday" should be "Monday" — days of the week are proper nouns and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: friend/hospital = general words (common). December/Christmas/Tuesday = specific names (proper).

questions.question: Fix the error: 'We sing songs on christmas.' Write the corrected holiday name.
questions.explanation: Christmas is a proper noun (holiday name) — it must be capitalized: Christmas.
